metadata.title: Every Remotion Move, One Logo.
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Italian. claude-liam channel. Liam in for Bear.
metadata.note: Technique-showcase reel: the HAI H-mark animated every way Remotion knows. 22 beats. Audio-first, free pipeline only (Kokoro am_onyx). No ElevenLabs, no Higgsfield, no publishing.

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. One logo. Twenty techniques. I'm going to run the HAI H-mark through every motion move Remotion knows — so you can see which ones actually work.
Spring Entrance. Default spring, pop-in from nothing. Watch how it settles — is there enough weight, or does it feel cheap?
Overshoot Spring. High bounce config, squash on landing. Judge the rebound: does the squash feel physical, or is it noise?
Draw-On Stroke. The outline traces, then the fill floods in. The move is the reveal — does the trace feel like a pen, or a loading bar?
Per-Path Stagger. The H body arrives, the diagonal follows in a deliberate offset. Two shapes, one choreography.
Mask Reveal. A clip-path wipes left-to-right, then a radial iris. The shape stays fixed; the world opens around it.
Scale Zoom. Camera pushes from eight-times down to fit. Linear versus bezier — linear is blunt, bezier earns its landing.
Rotation. Pivot entrance, then a slow continuous hold. The mark just... turns. Judge whether that rest feels meditative or lazy.
Skew And Shear. The mark leans in like italic type, then releases. Dynamic tension, or just wobble?
Opacity Through Blur. Fades in while the blur resolves. The mark arrives out of focus and sharpens into ink.
Color Interpolation. Ink to terracotta and back. This is the one color beat — labeled as a treatment, not brand law. Watch whether the orange reads as signal or noise.
Kinetic Grid. The mark tiled across the frame, staggered ripple on delay. Grid becomes texture; texture becomes rhythm.
Glitch Slices. Horizontal slice offsets for eight frames, then snap clean. Aggressive, intentional, gone.
Trail Echo. Low-opacity copies chase a fast slide. Motion stays on the mark longer than the mark does.
Noise Wobble. Sine-driven micro-jitter that settles into stillness. The question: does the mark feel alive, or anxious?
Elastic Physics. Drop-in with scale-Y squash and rebound. The mark hits the floor and means it.
Orbit Parts. The two paths split, orbit each other, then rejoin into the mark. Separation and reunion — worth it only if the reunion lands with weight.
Card Flip. Perspective transform, rotate-Y. The logo on both faces of the card. Judge whether the flip reads as depth or just a squash.
Shadow Play. The shadow animates independently — stretching ahead, then snapping back to rejoin the mark. Detached, then grounded.
Composer Summon. The mark arrives from the Claude composer — typed as a prompt, sent with a terracotta spark. The interface is the origin.
Exit Family. Three quick exits back-to-back: shrink-spin, blur-out, mask-close. Exits are underrated. These three are the ones worth keeping.
Twenty techniques, one mark. Now take this prompt, run it on your own logo, and see which moves survive contact with your actual brand.
Liam, in for Bear.
